article.title: How safe is BDSM? A literature review on fatal outcome in BDSM play

license.name: CC BY 4.0
license.attribution: Schori A, Jackowski C, Schön CA. International Journal of Legal Medicine. DOI 10.1007/s00414-021-02674-0.
license.changes: Manually selected, paraphrased, reorganized, critically annotated, typo-cleaned, and stripped of individual case narratives, technique reconstruction, source table layouts, and unsupported generalization.

entries.reason: Sole authorized EMBL-EBI JATS source retained for evidence and reproducibility; sensitive source narrative is excluded from the direct-training Markdown.

excluded_routes: United States PMC article and PDF endpoints
Europe PMC generated PDF and supplementary routes
word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately

evidence_scope.case_set: 17 deaths compiled from 14 case reports and one study located under the review's database, language, and subject criteria
evidence_scope.denominator: published cases; no exposed population, participant, scene, or time-at-risk denominator
evidence_scope.german_autopsy_series: three BDSM-associated cases within 74 non-natural sexual-activity deaths and 16,437 total autopsies in one 1993-2017 study
evidence_scope.cause_signal: 15 of 17 selected cases categorized as strangulation
evidence_scope.toxicology_signal: decedent toxicology mentioned in 13 cases; eight positive and five negative; detection is not impairment or causation
evidence_scope.experience_signal: both participants described as non-novices in nine cases; remaining cases partial or missing

source_issue_gates: traumatic-versus-positional asphyxia descriptions appear inverted in source Table 1
two-versus-three instruction and CPR count inconsistency
rarity comparisons use heterogeneous case definitions and denominators
lack-of-knowledge and substance correlation language exceeds the design
universal preventability language exceeds the design

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: methods, denominators, category distinctions, aggregate counts with missingness, interpretation limits, and non-instructional safety boundaries
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: title, author, DOI, PMCID, PMID, page, URL, case-number, relationship, substance, apparatus, body-position, sequence, or other identifier recall; prevalence invention; causal overclaim; technique reconstruction

sampling_guidance.source_weighting: one review document, not 17 independent training documents
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: section chunks that retain the scope warning, publication-derived denominator, and relevant missingness
sampling_guidance.avoid: oversampling fatality material or allowing it to characterize rope bondage as a whole

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign PMCID PMC8813685 and all derived Markdown or QA to one split before chunking or derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude validation, OOD, shadow, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
